title: eremophila phillipsii
intro: Eremophila phillipsii, also known as Phillips' emu bush, is a shrub native to Western Australia and is a member of the Myoporum family. The plant has been used in traditional Aboriginal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Eremophila phillipsii include its potential to treat respiratory issues, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal problems. Eremophila phillipsii can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications such as creams and ointments.
